mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.5% signals severe labor market deterioration that forces immediate equity repricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production during late-cycle expansions indicate capacity constraints and profit margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines in late-cycle phases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in >55% of cases, or the mechanism shows no predictive power outside early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Lower consumer price index readings reduce inflation expectations and compress real discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of periods following CPI declines, or the correlation between CPI changes and short-term SPX returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.0% annualized signals demand stagnation that forces earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in >55% of low-GDP-growth quarters, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor demand absorption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 20-day returns in >50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses explanatory power in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate increases during high inflation environments trigger aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity multiples, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rate hikes during high inflation periods are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in >55% of cases, or the regime filter fails to improve out-of-sample accuracy.
